title: Learning Loop Retrospective
prompt: After three exercise attempts, design the next improvement loop for this course. Include what memory should preserve, what feedback should be submitted, what improvement signals should be inspected, how a small cohort review should check the change, and what should not be auto-edited.
hints: The server improves future performance through retrieval, feedback, practice, cohort review, and maintainer-reviewed course edits.

model_answer.summary: The next loop should preserve useful memory, gather feedback, inspect aggregate signals, and route course edits through human review.
model_answer.work: Memory should preserve the enrollment_key, completed lesson_slug and exercise_slug handles, attempt scores, repeated misses, useful feedback, and the learner's next recommended work. Ask the LLM to submit feedback when an exercise is confusing or helpful, then inspect get-course-improvement-signals for repeated failures, low confidence, common rubric misses, and aggregate comments. Run a small cohort in course-lab with different student and reviewer profiles before changing lessons. Do not auto-edit course content from raw feedback; proposed changes should be maintainer review only, because public feedback can be noisy or poison the course.
checks: Confirm get-learning-memory returns stable enrollment_key, lesson_slug, and exercise_slug history.
Review get-course-improvement-signals for repeated misses before editing content.
Run a cohort course-lab pass with at least several simulated students.
Have a reviewer compare changed lessons against source references.
Verify proposed edits are not auto-applied from untrusted feedback.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It keeps the learning loop evidence-based while protecting the course from untrusted automatic rewrites.
common_misses: Saving vague memories without stable lesson or exercise handles.
Treating one complaint as enough evidence for a course edit.
Skipping cohort review after changing instructions.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by naming what memory preserves, what feedback collects, which aggregate signals matter, how course-lab cohort review works, and what must not be auto-edited.
